name: mvesuvio

documentation:
# Mantid VESUVIO

[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/mantidproject/vesuvio/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/mantidproject/vesuvio?branch=main)

This repository contains:
- `mvesuvio` package containing the Optimized NCP analysis procedures, published nightly.
- Vesuvio calibration script

## Install mvesuvio package

### For stand-alone (non-conda) Mantid installation
Currently this method is tested up until Mantid 6.8. 

The easiest way to install the package is to install it through pip.
To pip install mvesuvio, follow these [instructions](https://docs.mantidproject.org/nightly/concepts/PipInstall.html) and replace `<insert_package_name>` with `mvesuvio`.

Then open mantid workbench and in the editor run the following script:

```
import mvesuvio as mv
mv.set_config()
```

You will see that the output shows two directories: one for the inputs file and another for the instrument parameters (IP) file.

Both of these directories sit inside a default location of a `.mvesuvio` folder.

The `.` in front of the directory name means this folder might be hidden by your OS, so you might have to turn on the option of showing hidden folders.

You should place of your IP files inside this IP directory, you will notice that I have placed some example files in there already.

The other directory in the output of the config command tells you which inputs file is being used for running the analysis.

You should go to the directory of the inputs file and copy-and-paste this file into any directory inside your desktop where you want to run the analysis for a given sample.

And that's the end of the installation and setup!

## Using mvesuvio

The easiest way to run the analysis scripts is to start with an inputs file and run it from inside mantid workbench.

Open mantid workbench and navigate to the directory of the inputs file you copy-and-pasted in the last section.

**Warning**: Do not run the analysis routine on the default location for the inputs file. This will clutter the default `.mvesuvio` folder and may cause problems later down the line.

Now you can alter the inputs of the file as you see fit. When you are finished, press run. This will run the routine and create a new directory for the output files inside the parent directory of the inputs file.

To run a new sample, simply copy-and-paste one of the inputs file into any directory you see fit, change the inputs and press run.

## Alternative Installation and Usage (Conda and CLI)
We also provide an alternative conda installation for users wanting to use mantid workbench inside a conda environment.

### Install mamba

To use the `mvesuvio` package you will need to use the `conda` package manager (or preferably  `mamba`, a much faster implementation of `conda`).

This is also the recommended best practice way of using the mantid packages.

To download and install mamba:
- https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/mamba-installation.html

### Create and activate an environment for mantidworkbench and mvesuvio

Firstly create a conda environment where `mvesuvio` is going to be installed:
- `mamba create -n <environment_name>`

Next activate the environment you created:
- `mamba activate <environment_name>`

Install mantid workbench (the version is pinned to 6.8 for now):
- `mamba install mantidworkbench=6.8`

Finally, install `mvesuvio` through the mantid channel:
- `mamba install -c mantid/label/nightly mvesuvio`

Start workbench using the command line:
- `workbench`

### Using mvesuvio via the command line (CLI)
If using a conda installation, the Command Line Interface (CLI) of the mvesuvio package becomes an attractive feature. 

This allows for setting the inputs file or the IP folder through terminal commands and run the analysis in the terminal (without 
the need for opening mantid workbench).

You can use `mvesuvio` via the command line. There are two commands available: `config` and `run`.

#### config

The `config` command has two optional arguments:
- `--set-inputs` - Sets the location of the inputs python file.
- `--set-ipfolder` - Sets the directory in which `mvesuvio` will look for instrument parameter files.

If any of these arguments are not provided a default location will be selected. These will be output on the running of `mvesuvo config`

Usage examples:
- `mvesuvio config --set-ipfolder C:\IPFolder` - Set instrument parameters folder.
- `mvesuvio config --set-inputs C:\Vesuvio\experiment\inputs.py` - Set inputs file.

#### run

The `run` command has one optional argument:
- `--yes` - If provided, this argument automatically inputs `Y` when prompted for user input.

Usage example:
- `mvesuvio run --yes` - Run the vesuvio analysis, automatically providing `Y` when prompted.
- `mvesuvio run`- Run the vesuvio analysis, will wait for user input when prompted.

### Importing mvesuvio in workbench

If you wish to write a small script using the mvesuvio package and have it run inside workbench, 
`mvesuvio` can be directly imported into the workbench.

In the workbench script editor you must first import mvesuvio:

- `import mvesuvio as mv`

After this you can set the config if desired, as above in the command line example. All arguments are optional.

- `mv.set_config(inputs_file='C:\Vesuvio\experiment\inputs.py', ip_folder='C:\IPFolder')`

Following the setting of the config, you can use workbench to open and edit the analysis input file created in the relevant experiment directory.
Once the inputs have been ammended and the file saved, run the analysis:

- `mv.run(yes_to_all=True)`

documentation: None or scaling factor

documentation: Flag to control wether empty ws gets subtracted from raw

class_variables.name: mvesuvio.config.analysis_inputs.ForwardAnalysisInputs.scaleEmpty
documentation: None or scaling factor

class_variables.name: mvesuvio.config.analysis_inputs.ForwardAnalysisInputs.transmission_guess

modules.name: mvesuvio.main
documentation: Package defining entry points.

modules.name: mvesuvio
documentation:
Vesuvio
=============

Vesuvio is an instrument that performs Neuton Compton Scattering, based at ISIS, RAL, UK. This code processes raw output data to determine
nuclear kinetic energies and moment distributions.

methods.name: mvesuvio.main.run_routine.Runner.run_estimate_h_ratio
documentation:
Used when H is present and H to first mass ratio is not known.
Preliminary forward scattering is run to get rough estimate of H to first mass ratio.
Runs iterative procedure with alternating back and forward scattering.

functions.name: mvesuvio.util.analysis_helpers.cropAndMaskWorkspace
documentation: Returns cloned and cropped workspace with modified name

functions.name: mvesuvio.util.analysis_helpers.maskBinsWithZeros
documentation:
Masks a given TOF range on ws with zeros on dataY.
Leaves errors dataE unchanged, as they are used by later treatments.
Used to mask resonance peaks.

functions.name: mvesuvio.util.analysis_helpers.extractWS
documentation: Directly extracts data from workspace into arrays

functions.name: mvesuvio.util.analysis_helpers.loadConstants
documentation:
Output: the mass of the neutron, final energy of neutrons (selected by gold foil),
factor to change energies into velocities, final velocity of neutron and hbar

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._fit_neutron_compton_profiles
documentation:
Performs the fit of neutron compton profiles to the workspace being fit.
The profiles are fit on a spectrum by spectrum basis.

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.loadInstrParsFileIntoArray
documentation: Loads instrument parameters into array, from the file in the specified path

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.loadResolutionPars
documentation:
Resolution of parameters to propagate into TOF resolution
Output: matrix with each parameter in each column

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.calculateKinematicsArrays
documentation: Kinematics quantities calculated from TOF data

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.convertDataXToYSpacesForEachMass
documentation: "Calculates y spaces from TOF data, each row corresponds to one mass

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._set_means_and_std
documentation: Calculate mean widths and intensities from tableWorkspace

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.filterWidthsAndIntensities
documentation: Puts nans in places to be ignored

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.errorFunction
documentation: Error function to be minimized, in TOF space

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._neutron_compton_profiles
documentation:
Neutron Compton Profile distribution on TOF space for a single spectrum. 
Calculated from kinematics, J(y) and resolution functions.

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.caculateResolutionForEachMass
documentation:
Calculates the gaussian and lorentzian resolution
output: two column vectors, each row corresponds to each mass

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.kinematicsAtYCenters
documentation: v0, E0, deltaE, deltaQ at the peak of the ncpTotal for each mass

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._replace_zero_columns_with_ncp_fit
documentation:
If the initial input contains columns with zeros 
(to mask resonance peaks) then these sections must be approximated 
by the total fitted function because multiple scattering and 
gamma correction algorithms do not accept columns with zeros.
If no masked columns are present then the input workspace 
for corrections is left unchanged.

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._remask_columns_with_zeros
documentation:
Uses previously stored information on masked columns in the
initial workspace to set these columns again to zero on the
workspace resulting from the multiple scattering or gamma correction.

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.create_multiple_scattering_workspaces
documentation: Creates _MulScattering and _TotScattering workspaces used for the MS correction

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.createMulScatWorkspaces
documentation: Uses the Mantid algorithm for the MS correction to create two Workspaces _tot_sctr and _mltp_sctr

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine.create_gamma_workspaces
documentation: Creates _gamma_background correction workspace to be subtracted from the main workspace

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._set_results
documentation: Used to collect results from workspaces and store them in .npz files for testing.

methods.name: mvesuvio.analysis_reduction.VesuvioAnalysisRoutine._save_results
documentation: Saves all of the arrays stored in this object

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.extractNCPFromWorkspaces
documentation: Extra function to extract ncps from loaded ws in mantid.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.subtractAllMassesExceptFirst
documentation:
Isolates TOF data from first mass only.
Input: ws containing TOF values, NCP for all mass profiles.
Output: ws with all profiles except first subtracted.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.switchFirstTwoAxis
documentation:
Exchanges the first two indices of an array A,
rearranges matrices per spectrum for iteration of main fitting procedure

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.ySpaceReduction
documentation: Seperate procedures depending on masking specified.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.replaceZerosWithNCP
documentation: Replaces columns of bins with zeros on dataY with NCP provided.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.dataXBining
documentation:
Changes dataX of a workspace to values in range of bin centers xp.
Same as shifting dataY values to closest bin center.
Output ws has several dataY values per dataX point.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.weightedAvgXBins
documentation: Weighted average on ws where dataY points are grouped per dataX bin centers.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.weightedAvgXBinsArr
documentation:
Weighted Average on arrays where several dataY points correspond to a single dataX point.
xp is the range over which to perform the average.
dataX points can only take values in xp.
Ignores any zero or NaN value.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.weightedAvgCols
documentation: Returns ws with weighted avg of columns of input ws

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.avgArr
documentation:
Average over columns of 2D dataY.
Ignores any zero values as being masked.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.weightedAvgArr
documentation:
Weighted average over columns of 2D arrays.
Ignores any zero or NaN value.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.normalise_workspace
documentation: Updates workspace with the normalised version.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.extractWS
documentation: Directly exctracts data from workspace into arrays

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.symmetrizeWs
documentation:
Symmetrizes workspace after weighted average.
Needs to have symmetric binning.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.symArr
documentation:
Performs averaging between two oposite points.
Takes only one 2D array.
Any zero gets assigned the oposite value.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.weightedSymArr
documentation:
Performs Inverse variance weighting between two oposite points.
When one of the points is a cut-off and the other is a valid point,
the final value will be the valid point.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.selectModelAndPars
documentation:
Selects the function to fit.
Specifies the starting parameters of that function as default parameters.
The shared parameters are used in the global fit.
The defaultPars should be in the same order as the signature of the function

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.selectNonZeros
documentation:
Selects non zero points.
Uses zeros in dataY becasue dataE can be all zeros in one of the bootstrap types.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.createFitResultsWorkspace
documentation: Creates workspace similar to the ones created by Mantid Fit.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.saveMinuitPlot
documentation: Saves figure with Minuit Fit.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.runMinos
documentation: Outputs columns to be displayed in a table workspace

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.runAndPlotManualMinos
documentation:
Runs brute implementation of minos algorithm and
plots the profile for each parameter along the way.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.resetMinuit
documentation: Sets Minuit parameters to best fit values and errors.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.buildVarRange
documentation: Range of points over which cost function is evaluated.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.plotProfile
documentation:
Plots likelihood profilef for the Migrad fvals.
varSpace : x axis
fValsMigrad : y axis

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.oddPointsRes
documentation: Make a odd grid that ensures a resolution with a single peak at the center.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.groupDetectors
documentation:
Uses the method of k-means to find clusters in theta-L1 space.
Input: instrument parameters to extract L1 and theta of detectors.
Output: list of group lists containing the idx of spectra.
------- Groupings

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.checkNGroupsValid
documentation: Checks number of groups selected for global fit is valid.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.kMeansClustering
documentation:
Algorithm used to form groups of detectors.
Works best for spherical groups with similar scaling on x and y axis.
Fails in some rare cases, solution is to try a different number of groups.
Returns clusters in the form a int i assigned to each detector.
Detectors with the same i assigned belong to the same group.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.closestCenter
documentation:
Checks each point and assigns it to closest center.
Each center is represented by an int i.
Returns clusters with corresponding centers.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.calculateCenters
documentation: Calculates centers for the given clusters

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.formIdxList
documentation: Converts assignment of clusters into a list of indexes.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.plotDetsAndInitialCenters
documentation: Used in debugging.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.plotFinalGroups
documentation: Plot of groupings of detectors.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.avgWeightDetGroups
documentation:
Performs weighted average on each detector group given by the index list.
The imput arrays do not include masked spectra.
--------- Weighted Avg of detectors

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.avgGroupsOverCols
documentation:
Averaging used when JoY workspace is already Rebinned and Normalised.
Selects groups of detectors and performs the weighted average for each group.
Returns arrays with the group averages.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.avgGroupsWithBins
documentation:
Performed only when mask with NaNs and Bins is turned on.
Selection of groups is done as usual.
Weighted average uses altered function to account for the unique format.
Several dataY points correspond to each dataX point.

functions.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.minuitInitialParameters
documentation: Buids dictionary to initialize Minuit with starting global+local parameters

variables.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.repoPath
documentation: Path to the repository

classes.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.MyLeastSquares

class_variables.name: mvesuvio.analysis_fitting.MyLeastSquares.errordef
documentation: for Minuit to compute errors correctly

Generic least-squares cost function without error.
This structure is required for high compatibility with Minuit.
